mission.title: Install and Configure Weaviate
mission.description: Weaviate is an open-source vector database designed for semantic search and data management, combining vector similarity search with structured filtering. It is ideal for applications such as recommendation systems, chatbots, and content classification, providing scalability and fault tolerance. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for proper resource allocation in production environments.

steps.title: Add Weaviate Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Weaviate Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install Weaviate using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add weaviate https://weaviate.io/helm-charts

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Weaviate Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Weaviate CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Weaviate to function correctly in your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/weaviate/weaviate/main/deploy/crds/weaviate.io_weaviateclasses.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/weaviate/weaviate/main/deploy/crds/weaviate.io_weaviate.yaml

```

> Installs the Weaviate class CRD.
> Installs the Weaviate instance CRD.

steps.title: Install Weaviate using Helm
steps.description:
This command installs Weaviate in the specified namespace with the desired version.

```bash

helm install weaviate weaviate/weaviate --namespace weaviate --create-namespace --version 1.36.2

```

> Installs Weaviate with the specified version.

steps.title: Create Weaviate Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Weaviate instance by applying a custom resource definition (CR) to your cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: weaviate.io/v1
kind: Weaviate
metadata:
  name: weaviate
spec:
  replication: 1
  module: text2vec-contextionary
EOF

```

> Creates a Weaviate instance with the contextionary module.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: weaviate.io/v1
kind: Weaviate
metadata:
  name: weaviate
spec:
  replication: 1
  module: text2vec-contextionary
EOF

steps.title: Verify Weaviate Installation
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Weaviate pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n weaviate

```

> Verify that the Weaviate pods are in 'Running' status.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Weaviate
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Weaviate installation from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all data stored in Weaviate.

```bash

helm uninstall weaviate --namespace weaviate

kubectl delete namespace weaviate

```

> Uninstalls Weaviate from the specified namespace.
> Deletes the Weaviate namespace, which may leave orphaned resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Weaviate
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This process will upgrade Weaviate to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl cp <your-backup-command> weaviate-weaviate-0:<backup-location>

helm upgrade weaviate weaviate/weaviate --namespace weaviate --version 1.36.2

kubectl get pods -n weaviate

```

> Backup your data before proceeding.
> Upgrades Weaviate to the specified version.
> Verify that the Weaviate pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Weaviate is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Weaviate pods may not have started correctly due to insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Check the resource allocation and ensure your nodes meet the minimum requirements.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the database'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The database service may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Verify the database connection settings in the Weaviate configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CRD not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) may not have been installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRD installation commands.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Pod is CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Weaviate pod may be crashing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n weaviate' for more details.

versionNotes.changes: Improved support for multi-tenancy and added new vectorization modules.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.35.x, ensure to migrate your data to the new vectorization module.

resolution.summary: You should see the Weaviate pods running with the command 'kubectl get pods -n weaviate' showing 'Running' status.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: weaviate.io/v1
kind: Weaviate
metadata:
  name: weaviate
spec:
  replication: 1
  module: text2vec-contextionary
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster with the necessary resources and tools installed.
